Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Willem Janssens Version: 1.8.0 Cegeka reference: Service Description - Modern SOC - DFIR - EU CONFIDENTIAL Service Description - Modern SOC Digital Forensics & Incident Response (DFIR) Table of contents 1 Service description 3 1.1 Definitions 3 1.2 Transition Services 4 1.2.1 Operational Readiness 4 1.2.2 RFS - Ready-for-Service 4 1.3 Managed Services 5 1.3.1 DFIR services 5 1.3.1.1 Incident Response Retainer 5 1.3.1.2 Incident Response Readiness assessment 5 1.3.1.3 Red Teaming assessment 5 1.3.1.4 Digital Forensics 6 1.3.1.5 Compromise Assessment 6 1.4 Governance 7 1.4.1 Process 7 1.4.1.1 Authorized Contacts 7 1.4.1.2 Operational & Escalation Contact 7 1.4.1.3 Decommissioning process 7 1.5 Service Levels 8 1.5.1 Emergency Response Time 8 1.6 Responsibilities 8 1.6.1 RACI 8

Section.name: Definitions
Section.Content: The following definitions shall have the meanings hereby respectively assigned to them. “Business Hours”: Monday through Friday from 8:00 to 18:00 CET/CEST excluding Belgian official holidays. “Horizon” : Customer Portal provided by Cegeka for reporting and change management “Initial Priority” : the priority which is pre-assigned automaticity to a Security Alert specific to a technology. “SOC/CSIRT Analyst” : Member of the SOC performing Incident Response activities “Security Event” : data recorded by a security system which represent security related occurrence, such as a failed or successful login attempt, allowed connection through a firewall, … “Security Alert” : a notification of a potential security anomaly as detected by one or more of the SOC detection technologies which requires further investigation. “Security Incident ” : a violation of security policies based upon a human reported event or a generated Security Alert. “Service ” : Managed Detection & Response services

Section.name: Operational Readiness
Section.Content: The goal of Operational Readiness is that both customer and Cegeka have a clear understanding of the governance and operational processes related to the DFIR service. Cegeka will perform the following activities during the Operational Readiness: The Operational Readiness will be formalized on a per project basis in a Customer Scoping Document . This document will include all the operational agreements related to the Service. The Customer is responsible for informing Cegeka of any changes which may impact the Service.

Section.name: RFS - Ready-for-Service
Section.Content: The RFS indicates that all the prerequisites are completed during the transition period to start the Service. This is the moment Cegeka will perform the Managed Services on the full contracted scope and invoicing will start accordingly. The prerequisites for the RFS are: Prerequisites The RFS date has a maximum term of three (3) months after signing the contract unless explicitly agreed otherwise.

Section.name: DFIR services
Section.Content: DFIR services are additional services not included in the MDR Service which can assist customers in more advanced incident response activities. The goal of the Incident Response Retainer is to perform the following: For Cegeka to able to effectively perform ‘Incident Response retainer’, the customer is responsible for: The goal of the Incident Response Readiness assessment is to evaluate an organization's ability to detect, investigate, and respond to security incidents. The assessment will identify gaps in the organization's incident response capabilities and to provide recommendations for improving their readiness to handle security incidents. During an Incident Response Readiness Assessment, Cegeka will perform the following activities: Each Incident Response Readiness Assessment is formalized in a Statement of Work document that outlines the scope, objectives, and timeline for the project. The goal of the Red Teaming assessment is to evaluate an organization's security posture by simulating an attack from a real-life adversary. The goal of Red Teaming is to identify vulnerabilities and weaknesses in an organization's security controls and processes, as well as to test the effectiveness of their incident response capabilities. During a Red Teaming assessment, Cegeka will perform the following activities: Each Red Teaming assessment is formalized in a Statement of Work document that outlines the scope, objectives, and timeline for the project. The goal of the Digital Forensics is to extract data from the digital assets, process it into actionable intelligence and present the findings. Digital Forensics can be host or log based. For example, artifacts can be found in filesystems, operating systems, network devices and other sources for the sake of reconstruction of the events. When working on a Digital Forensics assignment, Cegeka will follow a specific set of activities, including: Each Digital Forensics assignment will be formalized on a per project basis in a Statement of Work document, which outlines the scope, deliverables, and timelines for the project. The goal of the Compromise Assessment is to identify whether an organization has been compromised and if so, to determine the extent of the compromise, how the attacker gained access, and what actions they took while inside the network. Cegeka will perform the following activities regarding compromise assessment: Each compromise assessment will be formalized on a per project base in a Statement of Work document, which outlines the scope, deliverables, and timelines for the project.

Section.name: Process
Section.Content: For security reasons, only people identified as Authorized Contact are allowed to collaborate with our Cegeka SOC. Only they can interact with the Cegeka SOC. All other persons will automatically be prevented and will be notified towards the Customer. These are also the people who will receive the monthly report. The Authorized Contacts will be formalized in the Customer Scoping document . Any new Security Incident will result in a notification towards the customer Operational Contact (s). If any blocking issues prevents Cegeka from continuing the Service as agreed, an escalation towards the customer Escalation Contact will be done. The Operational & Escalation contact(s) will be formalized in the Customer Scoping document . At the end of the MDR Service, Cegeka retains the right to: At the end of the MDR Service, the customer retains the right to: At the end of the MDR Service, the customer has thirty (30) days from the date of termination to retain the right to:

Section.name: Emergency Response Time
Section.Content: Applicable Incident Response Retainer Definition The amount of time measured from the time Cegeka is notified by the customer to the time when an Incident Responder is reaching out to the customer through phone. Assumptions Calculation Per Security Incident Objective (SLA) 100% P1 2 hours P2 2 hours P3 2 hours P4 2 hours

Section.name: RACI
Section.Content: The table below reflect the responsibilities of Cegeka and Customer for the Cegeka Managed Detection & Response Services. Cegeka SOC CUSTOMER RESPONSE DFIR – Digital Forensics R C DFIR – Digital Forensics – access to devices I R DFIR – Compromise Assessment R C DFIR – Compromise Assessment – access to devices I R R esponsible: The party who is responsible for the execution of the activity. C onsulted: The party who must be consulted and must approve or provide input prior to execution. This party (partly) gives direction to the result. This is about two-way communication. I nformed: That is the person/party who is informed about the decisions, progress and results achieved, so that the next step can be taken. This is about one-way communication.
